AgentEvent.description:
Events emitted during agent execution

These events form the streaming API for consumers.

oneOf.description: Agent run started
Agent run completed successfully
Agent run failed
Hook invocation started.
Hook invocation completed.
Hook invocation failed.
Hook denied an action.
A rewrite patch was applied synchronously.
A background patch was published for downstream surfaces.
New turn started (calling LLM)
Streaming reasoning/thinking from the model
Reasoning/thinking complete for this block
Streaming text from the model
Text generation complete for this turn
Model requested a tool call
Tool result received (injected into conversation)
Turn completed
Starting tool execution
Tool execution completed

result.description: Legacy text projection retained for existing event consumers.

oneOf.description: Tool execution timed out
Context compaction started.

estimated_history_tokens.description: Estimated total history tokens before compaction.

input_tokens.description: Input tokens from the last LLM call that triggered compaction.

oneOf.description: Context compaction completed successfully.
Context compaction failed (non-fatal — agent continues with uncompacted history).
Budget warning (approaching limits)
Retrying after error
Skills resolved for this turn.
A skill reference could not be resolved.

error.description: Legacy display mirror for consumers still reading string errors.

reason.description: Structured reason for the failure. Legacy payloads deserialize as `unknown`.

reference.description: Legacy display mirror for consumers still reading string references.

skill_key.description: Canonical structured skill identity/reference, when resolution had one.

oneOf.description: An interaction completed successfully (terminal event for tap subscribers).

An interaction reached an external callback boundary and is waiting for
tool results before the session can continue.
An interaction failed (terminal event for tap subscribers).

Some streaming events were dropped due to channel backpressure.
Best-effort marker — the terminal event is authoritative.
Live tool configuration changed for this session.
A background shell job completed (or failed/cancelled/timed out).

BlobId.description:
Canonical realm-local blob identifier.

The identifier is content-addressed, but storage and GC semantics remain
realm-scoped.

CapabilityId.description:
Slug-validated capability identifier for skill requirements.

Replaces the legacy `Vec<String>` capability lists with a typed
namespace. Parsed at construction, so callers cannot smuggle invalid
identifiers into descriptors or requirements.

oneOf.description: Plain text content.
Base64-encoded image content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.
Durable blob reference used by persisted history/runtime state.
Base64-encoded video content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.

ContentInput.description:
Input content that can be either a plain text string or multimodal content blocks.

Deserializes from either `"text"` or `[{type: "text", text: "..."}, ...]`.
Provides `From<String>` and `From<&str>` for ergonomic construction.

DeferredCatalogDelta.description: Additive hidden-catalog delta metadata for runtime notices.

EventSourceIdentity.description:
Canonical typed source identity for streamed agent events.

`source_id` on [`EventEnvelope`] is a compatibility/display projection.
Callers that need source semantics must read this typed identity instead of
parsing legacy strings such as `session:{uuid}`.

ExternalToolDeltaPhase.description: Canonical lifecycle phase for external-tool boundary deltas.

HookId.description: Stable identifier for a configured hook.

HookPatch.description: Typed patch intents used by rewrite hooks.

oneOf.description: Mutate effective LLM request parameters.
Replace assistant text content in the latest model response.
Replace serialized tool arguments.
Mutate tool result payload before it is persisted.
Mutate final run output text.

HookPatchEnvelope.description: Stable envelope emitted for async patch publication.

HookReasonCode.description: Typed reason codes for guardrail denials.

authority payload; this string is a display projection.

This is the single identity carried across every surface — the wire parses
directly into this struct, tools receive this struct, the registry stores

HandlingMode.description:
Handling mode for ordinary content-bearing work.

`Queue` means outer-loop / next-turn handling and leaves the current run
untouched.
`Steer` means inner-loop handling and requests injection at the earliest
admissible cooperative boundary, while remaining ordinary work rather than
an out-of-band control-plane command.

InputSource.description: Source for an input event posted to an agent.

InputStreamMode.description: Whether this input/peer command should reserve a local interaction stream.

oneOf.description: Do not reserve any stream.
Reserve an interaction stream for the command.

PeerId.description:
Canonical runtime identity for a peer.

`PeerId` is the routing key: the router and trust store key by `PeerId`,
never by `PeerName`. Two peers may legitimately share a display `PeerName`
(per the Wave-B V5 dogma note), but their `PeerId`s never collide — the
underlying UUID is globally unique.

Constructed freshly (`PeerId::new`) for a peer minted locally, parsed
from a hyphenated UUID (`PeerId::parse`) when we've been given an identity
over the wire, or derived from a 32-byte Ed25519 public key when a transport
still authenticates by raw signing key.

PeerLifecycleKind.description:
One-way peer lifecycle notification kind.

These notifications are control-plane topology updates, not correlated
peer work requests. They intentionally do not create request/response
lifecycles and must never require an LLM-authored reply.

ResponseStatus.description:
Typed status for response interactions.

Mirrors `CommsStatus` from `meerkat-comms` — the comms runtime converts at the boundary.

CommsCommandRequest.description:
Typed wire request for `comms/send`.

Variants are serde-tagged on `kind` and validated structurally at the
deserialization boundary. Required fields per kind are enforced by the
type system; invalid discriminators (`source`, `stream`, `handling_mode`,
`status`) become serde deserialization errors rather than runtime
string-match failures.

Cross-field invariants that cannot be expressed structurally (e.g.
`handling_mode` is forbidden on `Accepted` peer responses) are checked
in [`CommsCommandRequest::into_command`].

oneOf.description: Inject input into the local session.
Send a one-way peer message.
Send a one-way peer lifecycle notification.
Send a request to a peer.
Send a response to a prior peer request.

CommsCommandRequest.title: CommsCommandRequest

PeerAddress.description:
Typed peer address: transport atom plus endpoint string.

The `endpoint` is transport-specific (path for `Uds`, `host:port` for
`Tcp`, agent name for `Inproc`) but is carried as a validated `String`
so the transport atom can be branched on without re-parsing.

PeerCapabilitySet.description:
Typed peer capability envelope for peer-directory output.

Extensions are intentionally opaque display/integration metadata. Core
routing, admission, and policy decisions must use typed fields such as
[`PeerDirectoryEntry::sendable_kinds`] instead of consulting this bag.

address.description:
Typed transport atom + endpoint. Replaces the prior free-form
`address: String` so the transport cannot be invented by string
concatenation at a call site.

name.description:
Display-only slug. Multiple entries may share a name; none share a
`peer_id`.

peer_id.description: Canonical runtime identity — the routing key.

Canonical runtime identity for a peer.

`PeerId` is the routing key: the router and trust store key by `PeerId`,
never by `PeerName`. Two peers may legitimately share a display `PeerName`
(per the Wave-B V5 dogma note), but their `PeerId`s never collide — the
underlying UUID is globally unique.

Constructed freshly (`PeerId::new`) for a peer minted locally, parsed

PeerMeta.description:
Supplementary metadata attached to a peer for discovery.

The peer's routing name lives in `TrustedPeer.name` / `comms_name`.
`PeerMeta` carries additional context: what the agent does and
arbitrary key/value labels.

# Serde

Both fields use `skip_serializing_if`, so `PeerMeta::default()`
serializes to `{}`.

description.description: What this agent does (e.g. "Reviews pull requests for style issues").

labels.description:
Arbitrary key/value labels (e.g. `{"lang": "rust", "team": "infra"}`).

`BTreeMap` keeps keys sorted for deterministic serialization.

PeerName.description:
Display-only slug for a peer.

`PeerName` is **not** a routing key after Wave-B V5: the router resolves
sends by [`PeerId`], and trust stores are keyed by [`PeerId`]. `PeerName`
is retained so human-facing surfaces (CLI, REST `comms.peers`, logs) can
render a recognisable handle next to the opaque id.

oneOf.description:
The peer admitted the transport but rejected our envelope at its
ingress policy gate (untrusted sender, full inbox, etc.). The peer
is still reachable at the transport level; policy denied us.

PeerTransport.description:
Typed transport atom for a peer address.

Replaces the old free-form `address: String` on `PeerDirectoryEntry` so
callers cannot accidentally invent new transports by string concatenation
at a call site.

oneOf.description: In-process routing within this runtime (no network hop).
Unix domain socket.
TCP endpoint.

ContractVersion.description:
Semantic version for the Meerkat wire contract.

Start at 0.1.0 — allow breaking changes during Phase 1-3 development.
Bump to 1.0.0 when the SDK Builder ships (Phase 5) and external
consumers exist. Before 1.0.0, minor bumps can be breaking.
ContractVersion.title: ContractVersion

ExecutionPlacement.description:
Structured metadata for where a tool or shell command executed.

This type deliberately avoids policy enforcement. It records facts that were
already established by the owning shell/tool/runtime path.

allowed_roots.description: Canonical roots that bounded execution, when safely known.

host_id.description: Stable runtime host identifier, when safely known.

working_root.description: Canonical working root used for execution, when safely known.

worktree_id.description: Stable worktree identifier, when safely known.

ExecutionPlacement.title: ExecutionPlacement

ExecutionPlacementIdentity.description:
Stable, non-path placement identity facts.

Working directories and allowed roots are intentionally excluded. Paths are
execution metadata; callers must not use them as the semantic identity of a
session, run, mob member, or operation.

host_id.description: Stable runtime host identifier, when a trusted runtime owner supplied it.

worktree_id.description: Stable worktree identifier, when a trusted runtime owner supplied it.

ExecutionPlacementIdentity.title: ExecutionPlacementIdentity

MobProfileBindingInput.description:
Profile binding input: either an inline profile or a realm profile reference.

Not `Eq`: `Inline(MobProfileInput)` transitively carries float provider
params (`temperature`, `top_p`) so `Eq` cannot be derived without
losing fidelity.

provider_params.description:
Non-`Eq` field: `WireProviderParamsOverride` contains float scalars
(`temperature`, `top_p`) so the struct can't derive `Eq` without
losing fidelity.

OutputSchema.description:
Configuration for structured output extraction.

When provided to an agent, the agent will perform an extraction turn after
completing the agentic work, forcing the LLM to output validated JSON that
conforms to the provided schema. The extraction JSON becomes the final
response text (schema-only) in [`RunResult`].

compat.description: Compatibility mode for provider lowering (lossy or strict)

name.description: Optional name for the schema (used by some providers)

schema.description: The JSON schema that the output must conform to

strict.description: Whether to enforce strict schema validation (provider-specific)

SchemaFormat.description: Schema format versions supported by Meerkat.

StructuredProviderExtension.description:
Typed non-semantic opaque bag for per-turn provider knobs that cannot be
fully typed without blocking a wave boundary. Explicitly marked
non-semantic and RMAT-exempt.

Use of this type is a deliberate boundary marker: content is passed
through without interpretation. Any consumer that needs to interpret the
content must promote the relevant structure into a proper typed variant
in its own wave.

Relocated from `meerkat_contracts::wire::runtime` into core so
`ProviderTag::Unknown { bag }` can name the bag without a cross-crate
cycle (adversarial review flaw 5). `meerkat-contracts` re-exports this
type so the wire path is preserved.

body.description: Opaque body. Non-semantic — never pattern matched across the wire.

key.description: Opaque key identifying the extension within the namespace.

oneOf.description: Forward-compatibility for unknown block types.

Wire projection of `ProviderTag::Unknown { bag }` — the typed
escape hatch for V3 legacy-row provider knobs that don't (yet) map
to a typed variant.

MobDefinitionInput.description:
Public mob definition input for `mob/create`.

This mirrors the public creation contract shape. Runtime-owned lifecycle and
bookkeeping fields such as internal owner/runtime bindings,
`session_cleanup_policy`, `is_implicit`, and internal-only profile tool
bundles are intentionally not part of this schema.

Not `Eq`: `profiles` transitively carries float provider params.
MobDefinitionInput.title: MobDefinitionInput

`Existed` returns the typed [`MobMemberListEntryWire`] roster snapshot so
public consumers do not need out-of-band knowledge of the Rust domain
`MobMemberListEntry` shape.

MobMemberListEntryWire.description:
Public roster entry returned by `mob/ensure_member`'s `Existed` outcome
(and other surfaces that want a typed snapshot of a single member). Mirrors
the public-facing fields of `meerkat_mob::runtime::MobMemberListEntry`
without leaking bridge-internal fields.

member_ref.description:
Server-resolved opaque handle for subsequent member-targeted calls
(work submission, cancellation, lifecycle). Replaces the binding-era
`generation` / `fence_token` pair on app-facing surfaces.

WireMemberRef.description:
Server-resolved opaque handle for a mob member.

Encodes `{mob_id, agent_identity}` as a single base64url-encoded token
that callers treat as opaque. The server resolves the current
`AgentRuntimeId` and fence token against the live mob roster on every
dispatch — clients never reason about `generation` or `fence_token`
directly.

Use [`WireMemberRef::encode`] to produce a token and
[`WireMemberRef::decode`] inside an RPC handler to recover the
`(mob_id, agent_identity)` pair before resolving against the runtime.

BridgeBootstrapToken.description:
One-time bootstrap proof exchanged between a mob supervisor and a
member runtime on initial bind.

Transparent over the wire (`#[serde(transparent)]` — a bare JSON string),
but carries a redacting `Debug` impl and has no `Display` impl so the
raw secret cannot accidentally land in logs or panic messages. Treat it
like an API key: read `as_str()` only at the comms/transport boundary.

MobMemberSpecWire.description:
Per-member spec for `mob/ensure_member` and the `desired` entries of
`mob/reconcile`.

Mirrors the essential, codegen-friendly fields of
[`meerkat_mob::SpawnMemberSpec`]. Complex sub-types (tool access policy,
budget split, inherited tool filter, override profile) are not on this
wire surface — callers that need that parity should use the non-declarative
`mob/spawn` method.

over the wire, or derived from a 32-byte Ed25519 public key when a transport
still authenticates by raw signing key.

arbitrary key/value labels.

Both fields use `skip_serializing_if`, so `PeerMeta::default()`
serializes to `{}`.

RealtimeErrorCode.description:
Typed realtime channel error code. Replaces the prior freeform `String` on
[`RealtimeChannelErrorFrame`]; all paths that mint a channel error pick
exactly one variant so downstream SDKs can match without string folklore.

oneOf.description: Incoming frame could not be parsed.
The first frame on a new socket was not `channel.open`.
The open token did not match any pending reservation.
The open token was valid but its TTL elapsed before use.
The client-requested role does not match the reservation.
The client-requested turning mode does not match the reservation.
The reservation requested a turning mode this host does not support.
The target already has an active primary channel.
The client handshake requested a protocol version this host cannot serve.

An input audio chunk's sample rate or channel count did not match the
format the provider session is negotiated for.

The open token was minted for a different realm than the one the
websocket session currently resolves to. Enforced server-side.
A tool dispatch exceeded its configured budget.
Generic internal runtime error (fallback when no better code fits).
Reconnect budget exhausted; channel is closing.
Realtime target did not parse or resolve.
No realtime binding exists for this channel (driver not ready).
Runtime layer returned an internal error that has no more-specific code.
Runtime driver is not in a state that can accept this operation.
Upstream provider session is closed.
Upstream provider session failed to open or progressed fatally.
Upstream provider session is temporarily unavailable.
Input modality is not supported by the negotiated capabilities.
No pending turn on this channel to commit or interrupt.
Observer channels may not send input or control frames.
`channel.open` is valid only as the first frame on a new socket.
`channel.commit_turn` is only valid for explicit-commit turning mode.
Channel is reconnecting; the caller must wait for a ready state.

W3-H: the mob member this channel was bound to has been retired and
its realtime binding released by the MobMachine
(`MemberSessionBindingChanged { old: Some, new: None }` effect).
Signalled only for `MobMember` targets; `SessionTarget` channels
do not encounter this.

Provider authentication rejected the connection or request. Maps
from `meerkat_client::LlmError::AuthenticationFailed` so the wire
surfaces a typed code instead of collapsing into
`InvalidTarget`.

Provider returned a content-filter rejection
(`meerkat_client::LlmError::ContentFiltered`). Typed so clients
can distinguish policy rejection from transport failure.

Provider rejected the request because the configured model is
unknown or unavailable
(`meerkat_client::LlmError::ModelNotFound`). Typed so clients
can distinguish model-catalog drift from realtime transport
misconfiguration.

Provider returned `InvalidRequest` for a shape the realtime
layer cannot classify more specifically. Kept distinct from
`InvalidTarget` (which is about parse/resolve of the target
itself, not the outgoing request payload).

Structured typed context carried on a channel error frame. Each variant
corresponds to a specific [`RealtimeErrorCode`] and lets clients match
the reason without parsing the message string.

requested.description:
Raw rejected client input. This string is diagnostic projection
only; accepted/default/supported protocol truth is typed by
[`RealtimeProtocolVersion`].

RealtimeProtocolVersion.description:
Canonical wire protocol version for realtime channels.

The version is bumped every time the on-the-wire shape of
[`RealtimeAudioChunk`], frame enums, or other load-bearing realtime
contracts changes in an incompatible way. Clients and servers handshake on
the string form; this enum is the single typed owner of the set.

typed [`RealtimeErrorCode`] on channel errors.

code.description: Typed error code. Single source of truth for branching in SDKs.

details.description: Structured context keyed to the code, when additional fields are useful.

message.description: Human-readable message suitable for logs and operator surfaces.

deadline_at.description:
Wall-clock deadline after which the reconnect cycle will be abandoned
and the channel will close with `ReconnectExhausted`. Derived from the
reconnect policy's `max_total_ms` on top of the cycle's start time. RFC
3339 encoded, matches `next_retry_at`. Present only while the channel
is actively reconnecting.

prosody_hint.description:
Opaque, provider-specific prosody annotation (tone / sentiment /
pitch hints). Additive field. Providers that do not surface
structured prosody leave it `None`; compactors and downstream
tools may use this as a hint only — the authoritative transcript
is still `text`.

oneOf.description:
The assistant output for `item_id` was truncated at
`audio_played_ms` because the user barged in. `truncated_text` is the
transcript prefix the user actually heard, as reported by the
provider; when the provider has not yet supplied a re-projected
transcript the field is omitted (`None`) and downstream projectors
should keep the existing transcript until a later emission fills it in.

RealtimeChannelTarget.description:
Target for a public realtime channel.

Two variants, one for each addressing mode:

- `SessionTarget` — standalone sessions (no mob-member continuity). The
  session id is pinned for the channel's lifetime; when that session
  ends, the channel ends.

- `MobMember` — mob-member continuity (W3-H / dogma #4). Identity is the
  canonical anchor, and the server resolves the current bridge session
  on every tick from the MobMachine's `member_session_bindings` map.
  Respawn atomically rotates the bridge session via the
  `MemberSessionBindingChanged { old: Some, new: Some }` effect; the
  channel survives without any SDK round-trip. A terminal
  `MemberSessionBindingChanged { old: Some, new: None }` closes the
  channel with `RealtimeErrorCode::BindingReleased`.

RealtimeBargeInTruncateFrame.description:
Payload for `channel.barge_in_truncate`.

Sent by the client the moment it detects the user starting to speak over
the assistant's audio, to tell the server what prefix was actually heard.
Field names mirror OpenAI Realtime's `conversation.item.truncate` so the
provider adapter can map straight through without re-encoding.

audio_played_ms.description:
Milliseconds of assistant audio the client actually played back
before the user interrupted. Must not exceed the true elapsed duration
of the audio content.

content_index.description: Content index within the item (0 for the primary audio/text stream).

item_id.description:
Assistant item whose output is being truncated. Matches the `item_id`
emitted in the public `RealtimeEvent::AssistantTranscriptTruncated`.

PeerCorrelationId.description:
Canonical correlation identifier for the peer request/response lifecycle.

On the sender side, `PeerCorrelationId` is both the outbound request
envelope id and the reservation key for any stream subscription. On the
receiver side, every response envelope carries it as `in_reply_to`. The
sender routes a reply back to its originating request by matching on this
id — no local id-translation map.

Wrapping it in a newtype prevents raw-`Uuid` confusion at every site that
fires a DSL input, inserts into the subscriber registry, or closes out an
inbound request.

PeerId.description:
Canonical runtime identity for a peer.

`PeerId` is the routing key: the router and trust store key by `PeerId`,
never by `PeerName`. Two peers may legitimately share a display `PeerName`
(per the Wave-B V5 dogma note), but their `PeerId`s never collide — the
underlying UUID is globally unique.

Constructed freshly (`PeerId::new`) for a peer minted locally, parsed
from a hyphenated UUID (`PeerId::parse`) when we've been given an identity
over the wire, or derived from a 32-byte Ed25519 public key when a transport
still authenticates by raw signing key.

render a recognisable handle next to the opaque id.

BindingId.description: Opaque slug identifying a binding inside a realm.

ProfileId.description: Opaque slug identifying an auth profile override on a connection.

WireConnectionRef.description:
Wire projection of [`meerkat_core::ConnectionRef`].

Pure structural shape — no `"realm:binding"` string form. Wave-b deleted
`parse` and `Display` on both the core type and the wire projection so
the colon-joined form cannot travel across wire boundaries.

WireAuthProfile.description:
Wire projection of [`meerkat_core::AuthProfile`]. Sensitive credential
material is NOT wire-projected; callers that inspect profile metadata use
the server-side `auth.profile.get` RPC or the
`GET /auth/bindings/{binding_id}` REST path; both return typed redacted
shapes. `source_kind` is a
discriminator for the credential-source variant.

provider.description:
Provider as a normalized string: `"openai"` / `"anthropic"` /
`"gemini"` / `"self_hosted"`. Wire types don't carry the strict
`Provider` enum so consumers aren't forced to update their
schema when we add new providers.

WireRealmConnectionSet.description:
Wire projection of [`meerkat_core::RealmConnectionSet`]. Returned
from the `realm/get` / `GET /realm/:id` endpoints.
WireRealmConnectionSet.title: WireRealmConnectionSet

WireRealtimeAttachmentStatus.description: Public live attachment status projection used by runtime and mob surfaces.
WireRealtimeAttachmentStatus.title: WireRealtimeAttachmentStatus

SessionId.description: Unique identifier for a session (UUID v7 for time-ordering)

SkillKey.description:
Canonical runtime identity for a skill.

This is the single identity carried across every surface — the wire parses
directly into this struct, tools receive this struct, the registry stores
this struct. There is no slash-delimited string path form.

SkillRuntimeDiagnostics.description: Runtime-visible diagnostics for skill health and quarantined entries.

SourceHealthSnapshot.description: Source health snapshot reported by sources.

WireUsage.description: Canonical token usage for wire protocol.

WireRunResult.description: Canonical run result for wire protocol.
WireRunResult.title: WireRunResult

BridgeBootstrapToken.description:
One-time bootstrap proof exchanged between a mob supervisor and a
member runtime on initial bind.

Transparent over the wire (`#[serde(transparent)]` — a bare JSON string),
but carries a redacting `Debug` impl and has no `Display` impl so the
raw secret cannot accidentally land in logs or panic messages. Treat it
like an API key: read `as_str()` only at the comms/transport boundary.

WireTrustedPeerIdentity.description: Typed external peer identity for public mob wiring surfaces.

oneOf.description: Recoverable Ed25519 public key string in `ed25519:<base64>` form.

WireRuntimeBinding.description:
Runtime binding for spawn requests.

First step toward identity-first mobs. Carries backend-specific binding
details at spawn time. `External` requires typed process identity; callers
do not supply raw comms peer IDs.

identity.description:
Typed Ed25519 signing identity for the external process. The
canonical comms `PeerId` is derived from this key after the wire
boundary, so callers cannot spoof an unrelated raw peer id.

WireRuntimeBinding.title: WireRuntimeBinding

WireRuntimeState.description: Public runtime state projection used by RPC surfaces.
WireRuntimeState.title: WireRuntimeState

BlobId.description:
Canonical realm-local blob identifier.

The identifier is content-addressed, but storage and GC semantics remain
realm-scoped.

BlobRef.description: Durable image reference owned by transcript/runtime state.

SystemNoticeKind.description: Typed system notice content carried in the transcript.

oneOf.description:
Auth lease transitioned to `reauth_required` — the active
`connection_ref` needs manual re-authentication before the next LLM
call can proceed (Phase 1.5-rev).

WireAssistantBlock.description:
Transcript block inside a block-assistant message.

Not `PartialEq`: `ToolUse.args` is `Box<RawValue>` for pass-through
fidelity (core invariant — opaque from provider to dispatcher), and
`RawValue` does not derive equality. Equivalence checks should
round-trip through serialization and compare the serialized bytes.

args.description:
Wire-projected tool-call args. Carries the opaque JSON payload
as `Box<RawValue>` to mirror the core `AssistantBlock::ToolUse`
invariant ("tool-call args pass through un-parsed from provider
to dispatcher") — see `CLAUDE.md` Rust design principles §3.
Allow-listed by dogma-blind-spots §7.

oneOf.description: Forward-compatibility for unknown block types.

WireProviderMeta.description: Provider continuity metadata for transcript blocks.

WireSessionMessage.description:
Canonical transcript message for public wire surfaces.

Not `PartialEq`: the `BlockAssistant.blocks` variant carries
`WireAssistantBlock`s which hold opaque tool-call args as
`Box<RawValue>`. See `WireAssistantBlock` doc.

WireStopReason.description: Canonical stop reason for transcript messages.

WireToolCall.description:
Legacy assistant tool call payload.

Not `PartialEq`: `args` rides as `Box<RawValue>` for pass-through
fidelity with `meerkat_core::types::AssistantBlock::ToolUse.args` —
opaque from provider to dispatcher, never re-parsed on the wire.
Equivalence checks should round-trip through serialization.

WireToolResult.description: Tool result payload in a transcript.

WireToolResultContent.description: Wire-safe tool result content that handles both legacy string and array formats.

WireSessionHistory.description:
Full session history in canonical wire format.

Not `PartialEq`: `messages` carries `WireSessionMessage` which
transitively holds opaque tool-call args; compare via serialization
round-trip rather than field equality.

plus the canonical comms `PeerId` derived from that key. MCP callers do
not provide raw peer IDs, and missing key material fails at the boundary.
